@@ -701,6 +701,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-nqyp","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"Release checklist for beads version {{version}}.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: {{version}} - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for {{version}}.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "{{version}}",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [{{version}}] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}}
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v{{version}}"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v{{version}}
git push origin v{{version}}
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v{{version}}

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations with proper codesigning (macOS).

```bash
# Build from source in mayor/rig (canonical build location)
cd ~/gt/beads/mayor/rig
git pull
go build -o bd ./cmd/bd

# Sign and install (macOS requires codesigning to avoid "Killed: 9")
# Uses fix-gt script which handles both gt and bd binaries
fix-gt

# Or manually sign if fix-gt not available:
# xattr -cr bd && codesign -f -s - bd
# cp bd ~/go/bin/bd && codesign -f -s - ~/go/bin/bd
# cp bd ~/.local/bin/bd && codesign -f -s - ~/.local/bin/bd

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows {{version}}
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.

 {"id":"bd-nv4g","title":"Add pre-sync validation hook","description":"Future: Hook integration to run bd lint before sync, respecting config settings.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:11.00402-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2026-01-02T00:04:24.394347-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T00:04:24.394347-08:00","close_reason":"Pre-sync validation hook already implemented; added comprehensive tests","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nv4g","depends_on_id":"bd-ou35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:22.791249-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip"},{"issue_id":"bd-nv4g","depends_on_id":"bd-t7jq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-01T11:19:24.008812-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip"}]}
 {"id":"bd-o18s","title":"Rename 'wisp' back to 'ephemeral' in beads API","description":"The beads API uses 'wisp' terminology (Wisp field, bd wisp command) but the underlying SQLite column is 'ephemeral'. 

This creates cognitive overhead since wisp is a Gas Town concept.

Rename to use 'ephemeral' consistently:
- types.Issue.Wisp → types.Issue.Ephemeral
- JSON field: wisp → ephemeral  
- CLI: bd wisp → bd ephemeral (or just use flags on existing commands)

The SQLite column already uses 'ephemeral' so no schema migration needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-26T20:16:36.627876-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-26T21:04:10.212439-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-26T21:04:10.212439-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-o34a","title":"Design auto-squash behavior for wisps","description":"Explore the design space for automatic wisp squashing.

**Context:**
Wisps are ephemeral molecules that should be squashed (digest) or burned (no trace)
when complete. Currently this is manual. Should it be automatic?

**Questions to answer:**
1. When should auto-squash trigger?
   - On molecule completion?
   - On session end/handoff?
   - On patrol detection?
   
2. What's the default summary for auto-squash?
   - Generic: 'Auto-squashed on completion'
   - Step-based: List closed steps
   - AI-generated: Require agent to provide

3. Should this be configurable?
   - Per-molecule setting in formula?
   - Global config: auto_squash: true/false
   - Per-wisp flag at creation time?

4. Who decides - Beads or Gas Town?
   - Beads: Provides operators (squash, burn)
   - Gas Town: Makes policy decisions
   - Proposal: GT patrol molecules call bd mol squash

**Constraints:**
- Don't lose important context (summary matters)
- Don't create noise in digest history
- Respect agent's intent (some wisps should burn, not squash)

**Recommendation:**
Gas Town patrol molecules should have explicit squash/burn steps.
Beads provides primitives, GT makes policy decisions.
Auto-squash at Beads level is probably wrong layer.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T18:23:24.833877-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T22:56:59.210809-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T22:56:59.210809-08:00"}
